message:
fuzz(path-component-validation): 15 boundary seeds across NameValidity

fuzz_path_component_validation drives a btrfs OpenFs through every
mutating syscall the FrankenFuse host layer exposes (create / mkdir
/ link / rename-new / rename-old / symlink + symlink_target) using a
newline-separated 7-tuple as input. The existing 3 seeds covered
"empty + slash", "long name", and "valid names" but never crossed the
255/256-byte length boundary, never exercised UTF-8 or NUL or control
bytes, and never split into half-valid/half-invalid mixes that
exercise differential per-operation behavior in a single run.

Adds 15 seeds covering:

  Per-`NameValidity`-class corner cases:
  * all-Empty: every name field empty in one run
  * all-ContainsSlash: every name has a `/`
  * all-Valid distinct: realistic distinct names per slot
  * mixed valid + empty: differential within one run
  * boundary 255 bytes (largest Valid) and 256 bytes (TooLong)
  * single-byte names (smallest Valid)
  * `/`-only and slash-leading/trailing variants

  Byte-class coverage:
  * `.` / `..` / `...` / `....` / `.dotfile` (filesystem-special)
  * UTF-8 multi-byte sequences (café, 日本語, emoji, accents)
  * embedded NUL bytes (validator currently accepts them — pin
    that behavior so a regression that suddenly rejects NULs is
    visible)
  * control characters (\t \r \x1B \x7F \x01..\x03 \x08)

  Symlink-target boundary:
  * 500-byte target with short names (within MAX_TARGET_BYTES=512)
  * exactly-512-byte target (boundary)

  Realistic: a typical filesystem-usage 7-tuple (document.pdf,
  My Photos 2026, .bashrc, drafted/renamed text files, plausible
  symlink target with `../shared/...` form).

Validated by a 16 s libFuzzer run: 9 102 executions over the new
corpus (the harness builds a btrfs image per run, so throughput is
single-digit-thousands rather than millions), no crashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
